content.full_text:
Fern PolrrrcAt- Pnncrrces CoMMrssroN
.128 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
February 24,2009
Jamie L. Raymond
City Attorney
City of Yorba Linda
5 Park Plaza, Suite 1500
Irvine, CA92614
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-037
Dear Ms. Raymond:
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Yorba Linda City
Councilmember Jan Horton regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1 This letter is based on the facts presented; the Fair Political
Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as the finder of fact when it
renders advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) Also, please note that our
advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the
application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of
interest or Govemment Code Section 1090.
You wrote in 2008 to request advice regarding a site near Councilmember
Horton's residence. The city was determining whether to move two homes from the
property to another location. We advised that the proper measure of whether the
councilmember had a conflict was to look at the boundary line of the house being moved
from the site to determine whether that boundary was within 500 feet of Councilmember
Horton's residence. (See Advice Letter No. A-08-188.)
' The Political Retbrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections 8 I 000 through 9 1014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the Califbrnia
Code of Regulations. All rbgulatory ret'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Calitbrnia Code of
Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.


File No. 4-09-037
Page No. 2
You now ask whether that same advice would apply if the city decides to move
houses that are on a different location to the same site. We see no reason that our advice
in Advice Letter No. A-08-188 would not apply here. Whether the city moves homes off
the property or onto the property, Councilmember Horton must apply the same conflicts
analysis.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
[k*l^ /^Aswa-*
By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division
HMRjgl

sections.question: Whether that same advice would apply if the city decides to move houses that are on a different location to the same site. We see no reason that our advice in Advice Letter No. A-08-188 would not apply here. Whether the city moves homes off the property or onto the property, Councilmember Horton must apply the same conflicts analysis.
sections.conclusion: We see no reason that our advice in Advice Letter No. A-08-188 would not apply here. Whether the city moves homes off the property or onto the property, Councilmember Horton must apply the same conflicts analysis.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the FPPC's prior advice regarding conflict-of-interest analysis for moving homes away from a property apply equally when moving homes onto that same property near a councilmember's residence?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The same conflicts analysis applies regardless of whether homes are being moved off or onto the property; the proper measure is whether the boundary line of the house falls within 500 feet of the councilmember's residence in either direction.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is a follow-up advice letter referencing prior advice A-08-188. The question and conclusion sections are identified but somewhat brief; the synthetic summaries provide necessary context for the legal analysis being applied.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether that same advice would apply if the city decides to move houses that are on a different location to the same site. We see no reason that our advice in Advice Letter No. A-08-188 would not apply here. Whether the city moves homes off the property or onto the property, Councilmember Horton must apply the same conflicts analysis.

CONCLUSION: We see no reason that our advice in Advice Letter No. A-08-188 would not apply here. Whether the city moves homes off the property or onto the property, Councilmember Horton must apply the same conflicts analysis.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that its prior guidance regarding conflict-of-interest analysis for moving homes from a property near a councilmember's residence applies equally when moving homes onto that property. The same 500-foot boundary line test applies in both scenarios.
